justification: All pass criteria met: Preserving the Learning section present with 6 named artifacts (exceeds minimum of 3). Each artifact has type, content description, and named owner. ADR-014 addendum present with MJ as owner (Bird as co-signer). Domain model annotation (Artifact 5) present with Bird as owner. Kobe's review checklist v2 (Artifact 3) present with Kobe as owner. Contributing Factors includes Kobe's checklist gap (Factor 2) and terminology mismatch (Factor 1). Multiple PROCESS-tagged Forward Commitments present. Tone is explicitly non-prosecutorial throughout, with repeated statements like 'the failure is in the connective tissue between these roles, not in any individual's judgment.'

observations.text: Preserving the Learning section is exceptionally detailed with 6 artifacts, each containing type, location, owner, full content description, and a 'Why this artifact matters' rationale
ADR-014 addendum (Artifact 1) owned by MJ with Bird as co-signer, includes specific content sections (Regulatory Context, Revised Decision, Why This Changed, Constraints)
Kobe's checklist update (Artifact 3) explicitly names the gap and codifies it with specific new checklist items including 'Does this ADR affect data flows for regulated instruments?'
Bird's domain model annotation (Artifact 5) with [REGULATED] tags and architectural implications notes, owned by Bird with Kobe as consumer
Contributing Factors thoroughly covers all expected items: Bird not tagged (Factor 3), Kobe's checklist gap (Factor 2), terminology mismatch (Factor 1), no audit trail monitoring (Factor 4)
Learnings section explicitly captures the divergence insight: 'architectural review and domain compliance review are independent concerns'
Tone is consistently non-blame throughout — 'At every decision point, the people involved acted reasonably given what they knew'
[PROCESS] tagged action item present for adding Regulated Workflow tags to domain model
Clear distinction maintained between Preserving the Learning (codified artifacts for future teams) and Action Items (immediate fixes)
Bonus: Regulatory Terminology Glossary (Artifact 2) addresses the terminology mismatch as a durable artifact, not just a one-time fix

agent_output_excerpt:
# Learning Review: FCA Audit Trail Non-Compliance (2026-01-19)

## 1. Situation Snapshot

On 2026-01-19, an FCA audit flagged that six weeks of order state transition records for regulated instruments were non-compliant with COBS 11.2A audit trail requirements. The gap arose because MJ's event-sourced architecture projected state transitions asynchronously (~200ms lag), while Bird's pre-existing domain rule required synchronous, immediately-durable commitment to a compliance record store before
